what_it_does: This file is a sent noticeboard message in the dragnet-development channel that lists three handled items: live-animated screen updates, auto-reload that now works, and microphone failure diagnostics that explain why voice input fails.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id item-22a6626f, type message, source claude_code, state sent, channel dragnet-development, and a single filed history entry.
The title is truncated mid-sentence ('now ') and contains line breaks.
Body item 1 states that all future changes will arrive as live animated updates with a fade and an "Updated" pill, and will wait until the user finishes typing.
Body item 2 states that auto-reload now works because the open screen is the new version that can update itself.
Body item 3 explains that the microphone fails because iOS browser speech APIs are dead stubs and all company speech services are offline, and asks whether to bring up an STT service next.
standouts: The message asserts a 'blanket law' that every future change will be delivered as a non-interrupting animated refresh.
It explicitly admits the previous auto-reload failure was caused by the open screen being an old version.
It gives a transparent, platform-specific root cause for microphone failure rather than hiding the error.
conventions: Markdown file with YAML frontmatter following a noticeboard item schema.
Uses a message type, sent state, author session id, and channel field.
Enumerates items in a human-readable list.
concerns: Title is truncated and contains literal newlines, which may hurt parsing or display.
Claims are forward-looking and cannot be verified from this file alone.
No links, references, or code evidence accompany the assertions.
Tone is informal/marketing-like for a ledger record.
notes: A development-channel noticeboard message announcing three UI/voice fixes and asking whether to stand up an STT service next.
questions: What product or screen is being updated?
What does 'blanket law' mean in technical terms?
Which specific STT service is intended to be brought up?
Is the claim about iOS speech API being a dead stub verified or inferred?
What is the author_session ch-s1mwveuc identifier tied to?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a sent message summarizing handled items, which matches; it also acts as a prompt to decide the next priority (STT service).
intention_signals: To communicate progress on UI refresh behavior and auto-reload, set user expectations for animated live updates, transparently explain the microphone failure root cause, and solicit a decision on bringing up a speech-to-text service.
novelty: It combines a feature announcement, a technical root-cause explanation, and a direct decision request in a single noticeboard ledger item.
contribution: It preserves a snapshot of three specific system behaviors (animated live updates, auto-reload, and mic diagnostics) along with the rationale for the mic failure, retaining both status and decision context.
summary_for_embedding: A sent noticeboard message in the dragnet-development channel records three handled items: live-animated screen updates, working auto-reload, and a diagnostic explanation that the microphone fails because iOS browser speech APIs are stubs and company STT services are offline.
intention_for_embedding: To inform the user of completed UI/reload fixes, explain why voice input currently fails, and ask whether to bring up a speech-to-text service next.
